stat: UK businesses using AI in some form but not yet integrated into their own systems (the 'shallow adoption' gap)
method: Recipe B (sector/segment cut) — combining two figures from a single primary survey with a UK business population count the survey did not itself apply

sources_used.title: Future of Work: AI in the Workplace (press release: 'Half of SMEs using AI, with limited headcount impact so far')
sources_used.publisher: British Chambers of Commerce and University of Essex ESRC Centre for Micro-Social Change (MiSoC)

sources_used.title: Business population estimates for the UK and regions 2024: statistical release

workings: BCC/Essex (March 2026): 54% of UK SMEs use AI in some form; ~11% have moved beyond generic tools (ChatGPT, Copilot) into deeper, bespoke integration with their own systems. Gap = 54% - 11% = 43 percentage points using AI without deep integration. Applied to gov.uk's 5,499,000 UK private-sector businesses (start of 2024): 0.43 x 5,499,000 = 2,364,570, rounded to approximately 2.4 million. Caveat: the BCC/Essex sample is SMEs specifically, while the gov.uk figure covers all UK private-sector businesses; since SMEs are the large majority of that population by count, the two are treated as broadly comparable, but this is an order-of-magnitude estimate, not an exact calculation. Neither source publishes the combined figure.
